items.title: A cognitive perspective for climate-smart agricultural policy in the Indo-Pacific region
items.abstract: Climate-smart agriculture policy is failing because it ignores the stakeholder cognition that drives political decisions. Cognitive mapping can make these worldviews explicit, creating a powerful tool for negotiation and more deliberative, context-sensitive policy.
items.authors: Rupak Goswami, Somen Nandi

items.title: The debate on ultra-processed foods
items.abstract: The strength of the evidence linking ultra-processed foods to adverse health outcomes polarizes the opinion of the scientific community. Two Perspectives published side-by-side in this issue of Nature Food explore these different views and their implications for food policy.

items.title: Policy synergies outweigh trade-offs for NH 3 and N 2 O co-control in China
items.abstract: Targeted strategies to simultaneously reduce NH3 and N2O emissions in agriculture are urgently needed. By combining high-resolution emissions inventories with policy scenario analysis, this study evaluates China’s national policies for abating Nr emissions and synergies between NH3 and N2O abatements.

items.title: Framing resonance and variation in social media: the case of slow food on Instagram
items.abstract: Sustainability-oriented social movement organisations (SMOs) and actors collaborating with them use social media to share messages that reflect or vary the SMO’s collective action frames (CAFs). Framing differences can shape public perception, potentially weakening movement identity, support, and influence. Limited research analysing multiple actors spreading SMO’s frames has neglected to study the role of corporatio…
items.authors: Ruth Areli García-León

items.title: Inflammation and nutrition-derived indicators for predicting amputation risk in patients with type 2 diabetic foot ulcers
items.abstract: BackgroundInflammation and malnutrition affect the prognosis of diabetic foot ulcers (DFU). This study systematically evaluated the impact and predictive value of inflammation and nutrition-derived indicators for amputation in DFU using machine learning models.MethodsThe study enrolled 1,052 patients with type 2 diabetic foot ulcers. items.title: Daytime nap and nighttime sleep duration in relation to dyslipidemia among type 2 diabetes patients in eastern China: a cross-sectional study
items.abstract: ObjectivesTo investigate associations of daytime nap and nighttime sleep duration with dyslipidemia among patients with type 2 diabetes (T2DM) in Eastern China.MethodsThis investigation was conducted in Zhejiang Province, China, from March to November 2018. items.abstract: ObjectiveTo analyze the relationship between the Metabolic Score for Visceral Fat (METS-VF), bone mineral content (BMC), and bone metabolism indexes in Chinese adolescents.MethodsThis cross-sectional study used stratified cluster sampling to select 1,206 adolescents (12–18 years) from six schools in Yinchuan (2017–2023). items.title: Application of indirect in-package surface dielectric barrier discharge cold plasma for post-harvest storage of table grapes
items.abstract: BackgroundTable grapes (Vitis vinifera var. Crimson Seedless) are highly susceptible to post-harvest deterioration, primarily due to weight loss, stalk browning, and spoilage by microorganisms like moulds. This study investigates the use of indirect in-package surface cold plasma within a passive Modified Atmosphere Packaging (MAP) to enhance microbial control and extend shelf-life of table grapes.MethodologyGrapes w…

items.title: A chemical and sensory comparison between commercial regular and alcohol-free beers from the Italian market
items.abstract: This study presents a comprehensive chemical and sensory comparison between commercial regular beers and their alcohol-free beer (NAB) counterparts available in the Italian market. Eight paired samples were characterised using physicochemical analyses, volatile aroma profiling via bidimensional gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GCxGC-MS), and sensory evaluation by a trained expert panel. The findings revealed tha…

items.title: Food safety knowledge and practices of stationary street food vendors: a case study of grassy park, city of Cape Town
items.abstract: IntroductionIn the Global South, street-vended foods play a crucial role in food security and economic sustenance; however, they also pose considerable public health risks due to systemic safety vulnerabilities. This study examined the food safety knowledge, compliance, and practices of stationary street food vendors in Grassy Park, Cape Town.MethodsUtilizing a quantitative cross-sectional descriptive design, data we…
